identification.question: What is the first step in designing a database?
What term describes a relatively simple representation of complex real-world data structures?
What term describes data modeling as being refined continuously over time?
What is anything about which data is collected and stored called?
What term refers to a characteristic or property of an entity?
What term describes how two or more entities are associated?
What term refers to a rule that restricts allowable data values?
What is a clear, precise statement describing a policy or constraint within an organization called?
In translating business rules, nouns correspond to what data model component?
In translating business rules, verbs correspond to what data model component?
What data model developed in the 1960s uses an upside-down tree structure?
What data model allows records to have multiple parents?
Who proposed the relational model and in what year?
What are rows called in a relational table?
What standard was introduced by Chen in 1976 for conceptual data modeling?
What notation uses diamonds to represent relationships in an ER diagram?
What notation is commonly used to represent cardinality and participation in ER diagrams?
What data model combines data and behavior into self-contained objects?
What language is used for modeling in the object-oriented data model?
What OO concept allows an object to inherit methods and attributes from a parent class?
What data model is used as the foundation of OLAP systems in data warehouses?
What type of database technology is designed for large-scale, distributed environments and handles both structured and unstructured data?
What abstraction level represents a user-specific view of data defined by external schemas?
What abstraction level represents a global, organization-wide view independent of hardware and DBMS?
What abstraction level is DBMS-specific and maps the conceptual model to database structures?
What is the lowest level of data abstraction that describes actual physical data storage?
What database design phase focuses on understanding what data is needed and why?
What database design phase creates a structured, DBMS-independent data model?
What database design phase maps the logical design to actual storage devices?
What is a single occurrence of an entity, represented as a row in a table, called?

multipleChoice.question: Which role focuses on how the database structure will be used to store and manage end-user data?
Which of the following best describes the purpose of a data model?
choices: B) To retrieve data from the database
C) To represent complex real-world data structures in a simplified way
D) To implement the physical database
multipleChoice.answer: C) To represent complex real-world data structures in a simplified way

multipleChoice.question: Which phase of database design produces standardized names, relationship types, and reduces redundancy?
Which relationship type is illustrated by 'EMPLOYEE manages STORE'?
Which relationship type is illustrated by 'EMPLOYEE learns SKILL'?
Which relationship type is illustrated by 'PAINTER paints PAINTING'?
Which of the following is NOT a reason why business rules matter in data modeling?
choices: A) They define entities and relationships
B) They serve as a communication bridge between users and designers
C) They store actual data in the database
D) They help designers understand business processes
multipleChoice.answer: C) They store actual data in the database

multipleChoice.question: What is the correct translation of verbs in business rules to data model components?
Which early data model supports only 1:M relationships and uses a tree-like structure?
Which data model was developed as an extension of the hierarchical model to support M:N relationships?
In a relational table, what do columns represent?
Which system manages relational databases and executes SQL queries?
Which of the following is NOT a component of an SQL-based relational database system?
Which model is described as a widely accepted standard for conceptual data modeling using graphical ERDs?
In the ER model, how are relationships visually represented in Chen notation?
Which feature distinguishes the Object-Oriented model from other data models?
choices: A) Uses tables with rows and columns
B) Combines data and behavior into objects
D) Uses diamonds to represent relationships
multipleChoice.answer: B) Combines data and behavior into objects

multipleChoice.question: Which OO concept groups objects that share similar characteristics?
Which data model is optimized for complex analytical queries and supports OLAP?
Which characteristic is associated with Big Data models?
Which level of data abstraction ensures security, simplicity, and application-specific views?
Which level of data abstraction integrates all external views into one global view?
Which level of data abstraction supports logical data independence?
What does the ER model use to represent entities?
Why can't you retrieve data directly from a data model?
choices: A) Data models are stored in a different database
B) Data models are abstractions that describe structure, not actual stored data
C) Data models only support SQL queries
D) Data models require OLAP to access data
multipleChoice.answer: B) Data models are abstractions that describe structure, not actual stored data

multipleChoice.question: Which of the following correctly compares the ER model and the Relational model?
choices: A) ER is low-level; Relational is high-level
B) ER uses foreign keys; Relational uses diamonds
C) ER is best for communication and design; Relational is best for storage and manipulation
D) ER stores data; Relational describes structure
multipleChoice.answer: C) ER is best for communication and design; Relational is best for storage and manipulation

multipleChoice.question: Which of the following is a key output of the Logical Database Design phase?
choices: A) Mapping to physical storage devices
C) Standardized names and reduced redundancy
multipleChoice.answer: C) Standardized names and reduced redundancy

multipleChoice.question: What must be introduced to implement an M:N relationship in a relational database?
Which of the following is a key concept of the Object-Oriented model?
Which data model is described as independent of both hardware and DBMS software?
What is the main limitation of the hierarchical data model?
choices: B) It cannot efficiently represent M:N relationships
C) It requires SQL to query data
D) It does not support parent-child structures
multipleChoice.answer: B) It cannot efficiently represent M:N relationships
